run_summary: Portfolio holds three positions, all down modestly 3-4 days in. TXRH and SHAK have intact catalysts (earnings/capital events within weeks/days), while DCO is showing sector sympathy weakness but the original earnings thesis is intact. New flags cluster heavily in post-earnings and sector-driven selloffs (housing, semicap, defense); most are RATIONAL or PARTIALLY RATIONAL with conf 3-4, which matches historical calibration. High-quality entrances are sparse. AEIS stands out as a genuine mispricing candidate (OVERDONE conf 3 on a financing event the market conflates with sector weakness), warranting a small exploratory entry. DCO's new flag contradicts the thesis_status slightly (thesis says 'intact' but sector weakness is mounting); will HOLD but watch closely. No other new flags justify conviction or exploratory tiers given their rational magnitude and lack of asymmetric setups.

entries.name: Onto Innovation
entries.catalyst: Sector-wide semicap selloff on Seagate AI-capex commentary, plus a same-day 8.01 filing
entries.thesis: ONTO is moving with the broader semicap cohort (ENTG, VECO, AEIS, PLAB all down 4-6% today and double-digits over five days), driven by Seagate's commentary that new memory factories 'take too long' to build — which the market is reading as a near-term AI-capex pause signal. ONTO's own 8.01 8-K today is generic 'other events' without 2.02 earnings or 5.02 officer items, so it's likely a press release or routine disclosure rather than a guide cut. The magnitude of the move is consistent with sector beta plus mild company-specific news.
entries.what_kills: 8-K reveals a customer loss, project cancellation, or M&A news; ONTO materially underperforms semicap peers tomorrow
entries.reasoning: RATIONAL conf 3 moving with semicap sector (down 11.47% 5-day) on legitimate Seagate capex commentary. Today's 8.01 8-K is explicitly 'other events' without 2.02 earnings or 5.02 officer items, so low severity. Magnitude of move is explained by sector beta + mild news. The call quality is too clean-and-expected to justify entry; if Seagate capex anxiety is overblown, the bounce will be equal across the cohort, and I'd rather wait for a +2-3% mean reversion day to re-assess rather than chase downside. No asymmetric setup.

entries.name: Kratos Defense & Security
entries.catalyst: Iran geopolitical escalation lifting defense sector
entries.thesis: KTOS's 5/15 8-K is item 5.07 (shareholder vote results) — annual meeting boilerplate, not a catalyst. Today's move is straightforward: ITA defense ETF +1.4%, Trump's Iran posture is escalatory (postponed strike at allies' request, with surrender rhetoric on Truth Social), and KTOS is a high-beta unmanned-systems play within defense. Magnitude is appropriate given its typical sector beta of ~2-3x ITA on geopolitical days.
entries.what_kills: Iran de-escalates suddenly; defense ETF rolls over and KTOS holds bid — would suggest unseen company catalyst worth investigating
entries.reasoning: RATIONAL conf 3 rallying on Iran geopolitical escalation and ITA defense ETF strength (+1.36%). Move (+4.09% 1-day, -4.86% 5-day) is straightforward beta to sector on well-known catalyst (Trump escalatory posture). The 5/15 8-K is just annual-meeting boilerplate (5.07). This is a sector-momentum play with low information content — if I want geopolitical defense beta I'd own ITA outright, not chase single-name volatility on a day when the move is entirely explained by the ETF. SKIP.

entries.name: Ducommun Incorporated
entries.catalyst: Defense sector pullback after positive earnings reaction faded
entries.thesis: DCO reported earnings 5/12 (2.02 8-K) and was actually +4.75% over 5 days going into today, suggesting an initial positive reaction. Today's -5.17% on average volume with ITA -3.2% looks like sector-sympathy giving back the post-earnings pop. With volume only 1.04x, this is not a fundamental re-rating — but the give-back may have further to run if defense weakness persists, because the post-earnings bid was the only thing supporting it.
entries.what_kills: If DCO holds the pre-earnings reference and defense ETF stabilizes, the give-back is complete and thesis breaks
entries.reasoning: Already own 4 shares at cost 149.01. This new flag is a follow-up on the original UNDERDONE thesis, restating that post-earnings strength is fading into sector weakness. Not a new buy signal — it's an update on an existing position. See position_decisions above for HOLD rationale.

entries.name: BridgeBio Pharma, Inc.
entries.catalyst: 8-K filed today with item 8.01 (other events)
entries.thesis: The 5/13 8-K (item 8.01 'other events') filed today is the proximate driver — typically used for clinical data updates, label expansions, or partnership disclosures. Combined with the 5/8 material agreement filing (item 1.01 + 1.02), there's a sequence of business development activity. +4% on average volume is a measured reaction — neither overdone nor underdone without knowing the specific announcement content.
entries.what_kills: If it's just an investor-day presentation re-filing, +4% is the full reaction
entries.reasoning: RATIONAL conf 3, +4% on average volume today on a 5/13 8-K (item 8.01 'other events'). Proximate catalyst is likely clinical data, partnership, or operational update, but the thesis explicitly acknowledges magnitude is 'neither overdone nor underdone without knowing the specific announcement content.' This is a 'read the exhibit' call, and the flag itself admits low signal — could be data, could be a presentation re-filing. Not enough conviction or misprint signal to warrant entry. If the 8.01 is positive data on Acoramidis, the move will continue and I can chase it at a higher price on confirmation. SKIP.

entries.name: Alpha Metallurgical Resources
entries.catalyst: Q1 earnings filed 2026-05-08
entries.thesis: Continued post-earnings selling after the 5/8 print (item 2.02) and Reg FD disclosure. Met coal pricing remains under pressure and the cumulative -9% five-day move plus today's -4.6% is consistent with a soft print followed by analyst cuts. Magnitude appropriate for a commodity producer in a weak pricing environment.
entries.what_kills: China stimulus announcement during Trump-Xi summit lifting steel/met coal expectations could sharply reverse this
entries.reasoning: RATIONAL conf 3, post-earnings drift in met coal name after soft 5/8 print. Down 4.57% today on 1.24x volume, -9% over 5 days. Magnitude is explained by weak commodity pricing and analyst cuts. The thesis is straightforward cyclical selling with a two-way China stimulus risk mentioned in 'what_kills.' Not an obvious misprint — the move matches the fundamental setup. The China summit risk is real but too uncertain to size conviction on, and waiting for clarity on Xi-Trump outcome is prudent. SKIP.

entries.name: Shake Shack, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Earnings + officer departure announced 5/7
entries.thesis: Two 8-Ks filed 5/7: one with 2.02 earnings, the other with 5.02 officer departure. The combination of an earnings print AND a management change in the same filing window is a textbook bad-news bundle. With the stock already down 32.5% over 5 days, today's -8% likely reflects continued institutional re-rating rather than fresh news. Magnitude appears justified given dual catalysts.
entries.what_kills: 5.02 turns out to be planned board succession or a controlled CEO transition with a strong replacement named — then the -32% 5-day move is overdone and a bounce candidate.
entries.reasoning: Already own 33 shares at cost 60.95. This is a follow-up flag re-confirming the original thesis (earnings + 5.02 officer departure on 5/7 as bad-news bundle). Flag is labeled LIKELY RATIONAL conf 4, correctly identifying the magnitude. Not a new buy signal — it's validation of the existing position thesis. See position_decisions above for HOLD rationale.

entries.name: Whirlpool Corporation
entries.catalyst: Post-earnings selloff + April home sales disappointment
entries.thesis: WHR reported 5/6 (2.02). Stock down 22.6% over 5 days and another -8.6% today — today's leg likely tied to the April home sales miss reported this morning (CNBC). Appliances are tightly correlated to existing-home turnover. Compounding catalyst, magnitude looks justified.
entries.what_kills: Mortgage rates pull back sharply on Fed/Iran resolution; April housing data revised; WHR announces meaningful tariff offset or restructuring.
entries.reasoning: LIKELY RATIONAL conf 4, -8.63% today on 2.25x volume, down 22.6% over 5 days post-earnings (5/6). Catalyst is compounding: 2.02 earnings on 5/6 + April home sales miss reported today. Magnitude is fully justified given appliance/housing correlation. The setup is clean but not mispriced — a 22.6% five-day move on earnings + macro data is what should happen, not an overshoot. If housing data stabilizes next week, WHR may bounce, but entering now on a >20% down move in a macro-sensitive name is chasing downside rather than fading. SKIP.

entries.name: Installed Building Products
entries.catalyst: Earnings 5/7 + housing data sympathy today
entries.thesis: Reported 5/7 with 2.02. Stock now down 25.6% over 5 days. Today's -6% likely pure housing-data sympathy (April home sales miss). The cumulative move is large for what is typically a high-quality compounder; magnitude reflects builder slowdown but watch for overshoot if housing data stabilizes. Worth a closer look than SHAK or WHR because IBP has a stronger historical track record.
entries.what_kills: Earnings actually beat and selloff is purely sector-driven; insider buying emerges; M&A pipeline commentary remains strong.
entries.reasoning: PARTIALLY RATIONAL conf 3, -6.27% today on 2.25x volume, down 25.6% over 5 days post-earnings (5/7). Catalyst is earnings + housing sympathy. Flag notes this is 'a high-quality compounder' with 'stronger historical track record' and suggests 'asymmetric entry 5-10 trading days post-print,' which is accurate — but we are only 2 trading days past print. The downside may not be exhausted, and the flag itself cautions against fading immediately. Will SKIP and revisit in 3-5 days if the stock stabilizes or if housing data rebounds.

entries.name: Hecla Mining
entries.catalyst: Silver +7% to multi-month high
entries.thesis: CNBC notes silver surged 7% to highest since March on AI/parabolic rally narrative. Hecla is the largest US primary silver producer — operating leverage on a 7% silver move easily justifies +11%. Earnings filed 5/5 likely already digested. This is rational commodity beta with a recent positive print as the floor.
entries.what_kills: Silver futures reverse on profit-taking; HL 5-day gain is already +17.6%, so positioning getting crowded.
entries.reasoning: LIKELY RATIONAL conf 4, +11.07% today on 1.66x volume. Silver +7% to highest since March drives HL operating leverage. Earnings floor (5/5) is intact. Move is completely justified — not mispriced. The flag notes 5-day gain is already +17.6% and 'positioning getting crowded,' which is a caution flag: I'm entering a momentum move after a two-week+ run, not fading an overshoot. If silver consolidates or pulls back 3-5% over the next 3-5 days, HL could be a better entry. SKIP here and monitor.

entries.name: Celsius Holdings
entries.catalyst: Earnings 5/7 + staples sector weakness
entries.thesis: Reported 5/7. -8.7% over 5 days with another -5% today, on 2.26x volume. Likely combination of post-earnings drift and consumer staples weakness (XLP and ELF also down). Magnitude consistent with growth-name multiple compression on any whiff of distribution slowdown.
entries.what_kills: Scanner data shows reaccel; PEP reaffirms support; Alani Nu integration commentary is positive.
entries.reasoning: PARTIALLY RATIONAL conf 3, -5.11% today on 2.26x volume, down 8.7% over 5 days post-earnings (5/7) plus staples sector weakness (XLP -0.96%). Magnitude consistent with growth-name multiple compression on distribution concerns. The setup is explained and rational — no misprint signal. The flag cautions that confirmation would require 'Nielsen scanner data shows decelerating Celsius units' — i.e., the thesis requires forward data, not just price action. SKIP and wait for scanner confirmation or stabilization.

entries.name: Insight Enterprises
entries.catalyst: Post-earnings continuation + enterprise AI narrative
entries.thesis: Reported 5/7. 5-day +25.2% — print was clearly a beat. Today's +5% is continuation, plausibly tied to enterprise AI adoption narrative (OpenAI Dresser commentary today). NSIT is an IT solutions/reseller play with direct AI infrastructure exposure. Magnitude rational but momentum is extended.
entries.what_kills: Extension into low-float momentum chase fades; gross margin in transcript turns out worse than headline EPS.
entries.reasoning: PARTIALLY RATIONAL conf 3, +5.3% today on 2.37x volume, up 25.2% over 5 days post-earnings (5/7). This is a post-earnings beat continuation in an IT-distribution name. The move is rational and the flag cautions 'momentum is extended.' Entering a +25% five-day move on the hope it continues into a larger AI narrative is chasing momentum, not fading an overshoot. If NSIT pulls back 8-12% over the next 3-5 days and the enterprise AI narrative remains intact, it becomes a better entry. SKIP here.

entries.name: Kodiak Gas Services
entries.catalyst: Earnings 5/11 + XLE +2.64%
entries.thesis: Earnings filed today (5/11). Stock +8.4% on 2x volume into a strong energy tape (Hormuz commentary, oil concerns). Combination of an in-the-day 2.02 print plus +XLE makes the magnitude reasonable. 5-day +10.9% suggests pre-positioning was modest, so post-print rally is real reaction.
entries.what_kills: Energy unwind on Iran ceasefire revival; secondary equity offering announcement.
entries.reasoning: LIKELY RATIONAL conf 4, +8.43% today on 2.15x volume on today's 5/11 earnings filing plus XLE +2.64% sector tailwind. Move is fully explained and justified. The flag notes 5-day +10.9% suggests 'modest pre-positioning,' so the post-print rally is real, but I'm entering a +25% (5-day) energy move after the fact. If energy unwinds on Iran ceasefire or if KGS consolidates 3-5% over the next few days, it becomes a better entry. SKIP.

entries.name: Hawaiian Electric
entries.catalyst: Earnings 5/8 with likely wildfire-related disclosure
entries.thesis: Reported 5/8 (2.02). -6% today on 2.76x volume despite XLU +0.94% — meaningful underperformance vs sector. HE still carries Maui wildfire litigation overhang; earnings likely surfaced reserve/liability commentary that re-rates the credit. Magnitude justified if filing has updated reserves disclosure.
entries.what_kills: Reaction is purely a soft EPS print with no incremental litigation news — bounces in days.
entries.reasoning: PARTIALLY RATIONAL conf 3, -6.07% today on 2.76x volume, underperforming XLU (+0.94%) on 5/8 earnings. Thesis is that the earnings deck surfaced expanded wildfire reserves, re-rating the credit lower. Magnitude could be justified if true, but the flag acknowledges the outcome is uncertain ('if filing has updated reserves disclosure'). This is a name with known litigation overhang; entering on a -6% earnings day without concrete visibility into reserve changes is risky. If HE stabilizes and the reserve commentary is manageable, it becomes a better entry in 3-5 days. SKIP.

entries.name: Veracyte
entries.catalyst: Unknown — empty catalyst signals, against-tape move in healthcare diagnostics
entries.thesis: VCYT up nearly 8% with 1.4x volume but catalyst_signals is empty — no SEC filing, no earnings window. XBI is down 2.3% so this is not biotech sympathy; it's idiosyncratic and against the tape. Could be a sell-side upgrade, conference presentation reception, M&A rumor, or guideline-inclusion news that didn't surface in the news feed. Without visible evidence I cannot articulate either a fade or follow-through case at confidence 3+.
entries.what_kills: n/a — need to identify the catalyst first

entries.name: Beam Therapeutics
entries.catalyst: Biotech sector + rate sensitivity (30Y at 5.1%)
entries.thesis: BEAM, CRSP, BBIO all down 4-5% with XBI -3% — small/mid biotech is duration-sensitive and 30Y at 5.1% (highest in a year) plus Fed hike repricing crushes discount-rate-sensitive names. No catalyst signals on BEAM. 5-day -13.6% indicates ongoing sector unwind. Magnitude is roughly 1.7x XBI which is normal beta for a clinical-stage gene editing name.
entries.what_kills: If a positive clinical readout or M&A headline hits BEAM specifically, thesis breaks

entries.name: WEX Inc.
entries.catalyst: 8-K with annual meeting vote (5.07) + unidentified 8.01 exhibit; possible PE/activist chatter
entries.thesis: WEX +5.5% with the tape down 1.2% and on AVERAGE volume (0.96x) is genuinely odd. The 8-K is shareholder vote results — that doesn't drive a 5% rally. Could be activist filing, takeout chatter (Magnum PE takeover news today might be reviving fintech buyout speculation), or a buyback authorization in the 8.01 exhibit. Without seeing the 8.01 content I can't articulate either direction at conf 3+: a buyback/strategic review would be UNDERDONE bullish, a vote-driven mechanical bid would be OVERDONE.
entries.what_kills: n/a — UNCLEAR

entries.name: Builders FirstSource
entries.catalyst: 30Y yields hit 5.1%, Fed hike now in futures curve
entries.thesis: BLDR is one of the most rate-sensitive industrials. Fed funds futures now pricing a HIKE by December plus 30Y at year-high yields directly translates to higher mortgage rates → fewer housing starts → less lumber/framing demand. -4.31% on below-avg volume is mechanical macro flow, magnitude reasonable. UFPI -4.19% confirms the housing-supplier sympathy. Not a mispricing — a textbook rate-sensitive reaction.
entries.what_kills: Yield reversal or dovish Warsh commentary stabilizes the group

entries.name: Jones Lang LaSalle Incorporated
entries.catalyst: No identified catalyst; XLRE weak but magnitude exceeds sector move materially
entries.thesis: Move is the continuation of a multi-day decline in a weak sub-sector (XLRE -0.7% on a risk-on tape), but a 7% single-day drop on 0.9x volume with no filing is unusual — either a delayed reaction to the prior earnings print, an analyst action, or news not yet in our feed. I can't articulate either direction at conf 3+ without identifying the trigger.
entries.what_kills: If no specific catalyst surfaces and the stock stabilizes tomorrow, then this was just illiquid continuation selling and the gap fades

entries.name: Saia, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Possibly Trump-Xi trade optimism + cyclicals reflation; no SAIA-specific filing
entries.thesis: Trump-Xi summit + Boeing/China order headlines are bullish for goods-trade-exposed industrials, and LTL is a leading freight indicator. But XLI is only +0.5% and SAIA's move is 13x the sector — either a peer (ODFL, XPO) reported something positive, a freight-volume data print landed, or there's a SAIA-specific item not in our feed. Cannot commit to a direction without identifying it.

entries.name: Extreme Networks
entries.catalyst: Possibly AI-networking sympathy with AVGO; no company-specific filing
entries.thesis: Plausibly riding AI-networking sympathy (AVGO +5.5%, ANET-adjacent demand narrative), but +8.5% on 1.37x volume with no filing typically indicates either an upgrade, a contract win not yet in our feed, or speculative pre-positioning. Direction unclear without identifying the trigger.
entries.what_kills: If trigger turns out to be a rumor or speculative AI-tag, fade risk is high once the broader semis rally cools

entries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Likely sympathy with KTOS; Anduril valuation overhang
entries.thesis: Co-moving with KTOS on what appears to be Anduril competitive concerns. Volume only 1.14x suggests this is sympathy, not company-specific news. Direction unclear without knowing the actual driver.
entries.what_kills: AVAV-specific bad news in an 8-K would change this from sympathy to fundamental

entries.name: Manhattan Associates, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Unknown — no SEC filings or news
entries.thesis: Sub-1x volume on a -6.9% move is unusual — suggests a thin tape selloff rather than institutional distribution, possibly from a specific seller or a quiet downgrade. Five-day -8.9% indicates this is continuation of a slide, not a single-day event. Without identifying the driver I cannot construct a directional thesis at conf 3+.

Could be Auvelity script-data positive read or pipeline rumor, but I can't construct a conf 3+ directional case.

entries.name: Photronics, Inc.

entries.thesis: PLAB -5.4% with SMH -2.6% is roughly the expected beta for a small-cap semi-supply-chain name on a sharp semi down day. Slightly elevated volume (1.25x) but no catalyst_signals — most likely pure sector sympathy. Magnitude is in line with what high-beta semi small caps typically do on a 2.5%+ SMH down move.
entries.what_kills: Discovery of a PLAB-specific catalyst (customer concentration warning, China export-control news given Trump's China trip context) would reclassify this as something other than rational sympathy.

entries.name: Vicor Corporation
entries.catalyst: Unknown — no 8-K, no earnings; possibly AI-power sympathy or unfiled customer-related news
entries.thesis: VICR is a power-management chip supplier with AI datacenter exposure (48V power delivery for GPU racks). +22% on no SEC filing and modest 2x volume looks like either a private news leak (customer win, design-in disclosure at a conference) or aggressive AI-power sympathy buying piggybacking on NVDA/SMH strength. The 5-day change of only 24.7% means almost all of this is today.
entries.what_kills: No follow-on news within 48 hours and the move fades back below $290 — suggests a single-day momentum/short-squeeze with no fundamental driver. Empty 8-K slate at this magnitude is suspicious.

entries.name: Dropbox
entries.catalyst: Delayed post-earnings re-rating
entries.thesis: DBX reported 5/7 and 5-day change is +5.6% — meaning the stock initially rallied on earnings, but today is giving back -8.3%. This pattern (rally then fade) often indicates either sell-side downgrade following deeper-dive, or a guidance footnote investors initially missed. Worth investigating because the inversion of initial reaction is unusual.
entries.what_kills: Move is just broad SaaS weakness sympathy with no DBX-specific re-read; bounces back tomorrow.

entries.name: e.l.f. Beauty
entries.catalyst: Pre-earnings positioning (5/20)
entries.thesis: ELF reports in 9 days. -9% on 2.5x volume with no 8-K is pre-earnings de-risking, possibly tied to weakening Nielsen/Circana data in beauty or sympathy with broader consumer weakness. Magnitude is large for pre-positioning unless someone has a channel-check note out today.
entries.what_kills: Buyers step in pre-print; sector rebounds; no specific channel-check note surfaces.

entries.name: Ralliant Corporation
entries.catalyst: Pre-earnings positioning + semi/electronics sympathy
entries.thesis: RAL reports 5/12 pre-market — tomorrow morning. +4.9% on near-3x volume into a print is aggressive positioning. 5-day +8.8%. Either leaked positive whisper or sympathy with semi/electronic-components strength (SMH +1.7%, VICR +22%). Asymmetric setup: positioning into a print at near 52-week extension is a fade candidate if guide is anything less than blowout.
entries.what_kills: In-line print causes immediate -5% gap; or VICR-style rally is just a sympathy halo that fades.
